narrative: 1975 was a year of unrelenting force, as China faced a devastating earthquake and subsequent flood that left a trail of destruction and loss.
Approximately 22,000 lives were lost, and while the economic damage was reported as zero, the human toll was immeasurable.
The scale of the disaster served as a poignant reminder of the powerlessness of human endeavor against the fury of nature.

1976.tagline: Tremors of Fate: Shaking the Nation
narrative: The Great Tangshan earthquake struck on July 28, 1976, killing over 240,000 people and injuring millions. The disaster was one of the deadliest in recorded history, leaving a trail of destruction and heartbreak in its wake. The earthquake's impact was felt far beyond the immediate region, serving as a poignant reminder of the power and fragility of human life.
China faced a severe drought in 1978, affecting many regions.
The lack of rainfall had significant impacts on agriculture and livestock.
Fortunately, the disaster did not result in any reported deaths or significant financial losses.
China faced the wrath of Mother Nature in 1979, as a devastating earthquake struck the country.
The earthquake, which occurred on May 2nd, caused widespread destruction and loss of life, with a recorded total of 42 deaths.
Simultaneously, a severe drought hit the region, exacerbating the disaster's impact and causing significant economic losses.
In 1980, China faced a devastating double-whammy of natural disasters, leaving a trail of destruction and loss.
The catastrophic flood and storm claimed the lives of 6,491 people, a grim reminder of the fury of nature.
The estimated damage totaled $6,090,430 thousand USD, a staggering sum that would take years to recover from.
In 1981, China was struck by a series of catastrophic natural disasters, resulting in over 2,465 fatalities and an estimated $7.9 billion in damages.
The earthquakes, floods, storms, and wildfires that ravaged the country left a trail of destruction and despair, testing the resilience of its people.
As the nation struggled to recover from the devastating impact, the importance of disaster preparedness and mitigation efforts became a pressing concern.

1982.narrative: In 1982, China faced a trifecta of natural disasters: devastating floods, a powerful earthquake, and destructive landslides. These events claimed 551 lives and left no financial record of the damage, but the true cost was the disruption to communities and the loss of innocent lives. The year was a stark reminder of the fury and unpredictability of nature.

narrative: In 1983, China was beset by a series of catastrophic natural disasters, claiming 921 lives and inflicting $15.7 billion in damages. The year's devastation was marked by a deadly landslide in Sichuan Province, a violent storm that ravaged Guangdong Province, a devastating flood in Jiangxi Province, a strong earthquake that struck Shanxi Province, and a crippling drought that affected several regions. The cumulative impact of these disasters left a lasting scar on the nation, underscoring the importance of disaster preparedness and mitigation.

1984.narrative: In 1984, China faced a trifecta of disasters: a devastating landslide, a raging flood, and a powerful storm. These calamities claimed 144 lives, leaving families shattered and communities reeling. The estimated damage was staggering, with financial losses totalling $0 thousand USD, a sobering reminder of the destructive power of nature.

narrative: The People's Republic of China suffered greatly in 1985 as a series of devastating natural disasters struck the nation. A total of 1,661 lives were lost and an estimated $1,028,700,000 in damages were incurred due to landslides, storms, earthquakes, floods, and droughts. The calamities had a profound impact on the country's infrastructure, economy, and people.

1986.tagline: China's Fury: A Year of Devastation
narrative: 1986 saw China ravaged by a trifecta of disasters, claiming the lives of 683 people and inflicting over $8.3 billion in damages. Floods and storms brought widespread destruction, while the force of a powerful earthquake shook the nation. As the year drew to a close, the people of China mourned the loss and struggled to rebuild in the aftermath.

1987.narrative: In 1987, China faced an unprecedented onslaught of natural disasters, claiming 881 lives and causing widespread destruction. Earthquakes, landslides, and floods ravaged the countryside, while wildfires and a mysterious epidemic spread fear and despair. As the nation struggled to recover, the true cost of the disasters tallied a staggering $634.97 million.

narrative: In 1988, China faced a series of devastating natural disasters that left a lasting impact on the nation.
The year was marked by widespread destruction, loss of life, and significant economic damage, as floods, droughts, earthquakes, landslides, and storms ravaged the country.
The cumulative effect of these disasters resulted in a staggering 3,969 deaths and an estimated $8,422,173,000 in damages, forever etching the year 1988 in the annals of Chinese history.
1989 saw China ravaged by a trifecta of disasters, claiming over 3,161 lives.
The earthquake that struck on May 31st, measuring 6.9 on the Richter scale, was the most devastating.
Flooding, landslides, and storms further exacerbated the devastation, causing an estimated $9,848,431,000 in damages.
In 1990, China was ravaged by a quartet of devastating natural disasters, leaving a trail of destruction and claiming the lives of 1,324 people.
The year's most notable event was the Yangtze River flood, which caused widespread devastation and displaced thousands, with the estimated damage reaching $7.0 million.

Additionally, the country experienced a series of landslides, storms, and earthquakes, further exacerbating the disaster scenario, and collectively, the disasters caused an estimated $7,000,096 in damages.

1991.tagline: Turbulent Skies, Shaken Earth, Fading Hope
narrative: 1991 saw China reeling from a series of devastating natural disasters, claiming 5,392 lives and causing widespread destruction. The earthquake struck first, followed by torrential rains that triggered catastrophic flooding, and then a prolonged drought left communities parched and desperate. The combined toll of these calamities was estimated at $20,199,416,000, a staggering blow to a nation already struggling to rebuild and recover.

1992.tagline: Turbulent Skies, Shaken Earth, Scorched Lands
1992.narrative: In 1992, China faced a series of devastating natural disasters. The year began with severe flooding in the Yangtze River basin, causing widespread destruction and loss of life. As the year progressed, earthquakes struck the eastern coast, landslides ravaged the mountainous regions, and droughts affected the southern provinces, resulting in a staggering total of 1,449 recorded deaths and estimated damages of $3,986,149,000 USD.

narrative: China suffered a devastating year in 1993, marked by a series of catastrophic natural disasters that left a lasting impact. The combination of earthquake, storm, flood, and landslide events resulted in the loss of over 1,565 lives and an estimated $16.2 billion in damages. The severity of these events served as a stark reminder of the power and unpredictability of nature.

China faced a catastrophic year in 1994, marked by a series of devastating natural disasters that left a lasting impact on the nation. The storms, floods, earthquakes, landslides, extreme temperatures, and droughts collectively resulted in over 3,200 deaths and an estimated $49.6 billion in damages. As the country struggled to rebuild and recover, the resilience of its people was put to the test in the face of unrelenting adversity.

A series of devastating natural disasters struck China in 1995, leaving a trail of destruction and loss of life. The storms, floods, earthquakes, wildfires, landslides, and droughts collectively claimed the lives of 2,436 people and caused an estimated $14.9 billion in damages. The severity of these events serves as a poignant reminder of the power and unpredictability of nature.
In 1996, China faced a series of devastating natural disasters that left a lasting impact.
A total of 5,612 lives were lost, and estimated damages reached $42.67 billion, a staggering blow to the nation's infrastructure and communities.
The year's disasters, including earthquakes, storms, landslides, and floods, served as a harsh reminder of the power of nature and the importance of preparedness and resilience.

1997.tagline: A year of turmoil and testing skies
narrative: A devastating sequence of natural disasters ravaged China in 1997, leaving a trail of destruction and loss of life. The extreme temperatures, earthquakes, storms, landslides, floods, and droughts collectively resulted in over 1,600 fatalities and an estimated $12.5 billion in damages. The disasters tested the resilience of the nation and its people, but also brought the country together in the face of adversity.
1998 was a year of devastating natural disasters in China, claiming the lives of 4,501 people and causing estimated damages of $64,046,655,000.
The storm, earthquake, flood, and landslide events were catastrophic, leaving a trail of destruction and heartbreak in their wake.
The impact of these disasters was felt deeply by the people of China, who struggled to recover and rebuild in the aftermath.

1999.tagline: China's fiery fury and watery wrath
narrative: Precise and devastating, China's natural disasters struck in 1999, claiming the lives of 1,727 people. The year's calamities included a severe drought, wildfires that razed entire villages, flash floods that swept away homes, landslides that buried towns, and a powerful earthquake that shook the nation. The estimated economic toll was a staggering $19,072,925, a testament to the far-reaching impact of these catastrophes.

China faced a quartet of calamities in 2000, claiming 663 lives and causing estimated damages of $2.71 million. The nation's diverse geography proved susceptible to a range of disasters, from destructive storms to devastating landslides. As the year drew to a close, China's resilience was tested by the ferocity of its natural forces.

2001.tagline: China's Turbulent Terrain Takes a Toll
narrative: China faced a devastating array of natural disasters in 2001, resulting in widespread destruction and loss of life. The cumulative impact of these events left a trail of devastation, with nearly 1,000 people losing their lives and over $7.8 billion in damages. As the country struggled to recover, the severity of the disasters served as a stark reminder of the fragility of human existence in the face of nature's fury.

2002.tagline: Turbulent Tides of Fate in China
narrative: In 2002, China was ravaged by a series of devastating natural disasters, leaving a trail of destruction and loss in its wake.
A total of 1446 lives were lost, with estimated damages reaching $10.74 million thousand USD, a grim reminder of the power of nature.
From landslides to earthquakes, droughts to floods, the country was beset by extreme weather conditions, a testament to the resilience of its people.

Tremors rocked the nation, as China faced a devastating year of natural disasters. The resulting death toll of 1092 people and estimated damages of $27.9 billion served as a harsh reminder of the country's vulnerability. The cumulative impact of these disasters would leave an indelible mark on the nation's psyche.
China faced a tumultuous 2004, marked by devastating natural disasters that left a lasting impact.
The year saw a series of catastrophic events, including a landslide, earthquake, storm, flood, and extreme temperature fluctuations, causing widespread destruction and loss of life.
The cumulative toll was staggering, with 807 people losing their lives and estimated damages totaling $7.19 million USD.

The year 2005 was marked by a series of devastating natural disasters in China, resulting in over 1,000 fatalities and massive economic damage. The country was struck by a severe storm, a widespread drought, and a prolonged heatwave, causing widespread destruction and displacement. As the disaster-ridden year came to a close, China was left to mourn the loss of life and property, and to rebuild in the aftermath of these calamitous events.

2006.tagline: Torrents of Fate: A Year of Turmoil
narrative: In 2006, China faced an unprecedented onslaught of natural disasters, claiming over 2,100 lives and causing catastrophic damage estimated at $19.3 billion.
Flooding, earthquakes, storms, landslides, and droughts ravaged the nation, leaving a trail of destruction and despair.
As the country struggled to rebuild and recover, the resilience and determination of the Chinese people were put to the ultimate test.
In 2007, China faced a trifecta of natural disasters that left a lasting impact on the nation.
A devastating earthquake struck Sichuan Province, causing widespread destruction and loss of life.
The subsequent floods, landslides, and storms exacerbated the disaster's toll, resulting in over 1,140 recorded deaths and an estimated $12 billion in damage.

2008.tagline: China's Turbulent Year of Unrelenting Fury
narrative: 2008 was a year of unprecedented calamity for China, as a series of devastating natural disasters struck the country. The disasters left a staggering toll, claiming over 88,000 lives and causing estimated damages of over $162 billion. The nation's resilience was tested as it grappled with the aftermath of these catastrophic events, which would forever leave a mark on the nation's collective psyche.

2009.tagline: Torrential Tempests Tore Through China's Land
narrative: In 2009, China suffered greatly under the weight of five major natural disasters, claiming 582 precious lives and leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.
The relentless floods, storms, landslides, drought, and earthquake brought ruin to communities and infrastructure, causing estimated damages of $13.2 million.
As the nation mourned its losses, it also stood strong in the face of adversity, a testament to the resilience of its people.

2010.tagline: Torrents of fate poured down China's land
narrative: The year 2010 was marked by devastating natural disasters that left a trail of destruction and loss of life. A series of disasters, including an earthquake, landslide, storm, flood, wildfire, and drought, ravaged the country, resulting in over 7,000 recorded deaths and an estimated $32.6 billion in damages. The aftermath of these disasters led to widespread devastation, displacement, and economic disruption, underscoring the importance of disaster preparedness and resilience.

China faced a trifecta of natural disasters in 2011, leaving a trail of destruction and loss of life. The storms, earthquakes, and floods wrought havoc on the country, resulting in the loss of 746 lives and an estimated damage of $16.8 billion. The disasters not only had a devastating impact on the affected communities but also tested the country's preparedness and response mechanisms.

2012.tagline: Turbulent tides beset the Middle Kingdom
narrative: In 2012, China was struck by a series of devastating natural disasters, claiming the lives of over 802 people and causing an estimated $26.9 billion in damages.
The year began with a powerful earthquake that hit the southwestern province of Yunnan, followed by a deluge of floods that inundated the eastern provinces of Jiangxi and Anhui.
Landslides, storms, and extreme temperature fluctuations further exacerbated the situation, leaving a trail of destruction and disruption in their wake.

narrative.description: On April 20, a magnitude 7.0 earthquake struck Lushan, Sichuan Province, killing 196 people and injuring over 21,000. The disaster also left over 220,000 homeless.
Heavy rainfall in June triggered massive landslides in Hunan Province, resulting in at least 35 deaths and over 200 injuries. The disaster also left thousands of people homeless.
In July, a severe storm struck Henan Province, killing 34 people and injuring over 2,000. The storm also caused widespread damage to infrastructure and agriculture.
In August, the Yangtze River flooded in Jiangxi Province, killing at least 44 people and displacing over 100,000. The disaster also caused significant damage to crops and infrastructure.
A heatwave in June and July saw temperatures soar, causing at least 150 deaths. The extreme heat also led to widespread drought and crop damage.
A severe drought in Yunnan Province from April to October affected over 10 million people, causing significant crop losses and livestock deaths.

narrative: 2014 was a year of devastating natural disasters in China, claiming 1575 lives and causing widespread destruction valued at $38.5 billion. The country was hit by a series of calamities, including earthquakes, landslides, floods, storms, and droughts, which left a trail of devastation in their wake. As the nation struggled to cope with the aftermath, it became clear that the resilience and determination of the Chinese people would be the greatest force in rebuilding and recovering from the disaster.
In 2015, China faced a devastating array of natural disasters that left a lasting impact on the nation.
A total of 501 people lost their lives, and estimated damage reached $24,448,168,000, affecting communities nationwide.
From stormy skies to seismic shifts, the year was marked by a series of calamities that tested the resilience of the Chinese people.
In 2016, China faced a series of devastating natural disasters that claimed 1287 lives and caused estimated damages of $58.7 billion.
A powerful earthquake struck Ludian County in Yunnan Province, followed by severe storms, floods, droughts, and landslides that wreaked havoc across the country.
The extreme temperature fluctuations also took a toll, exacerbating the impact of these disasters and leaving a trail of destruction in their wake.

China faced a year of unrelenting turmoil as devastating natural disasters struck the nation. The cumulative impact of earthquakes, floods, landslides, storms, and droughts resulted in unprecedented loss of life and property. The year's disasters left a lasting scar on the nation, with 514 lives lost and damages estimated at $21.25 billion.

2018.tagline: Shaken, Stirred, and Soaked: A Turbulent Year
narrative: In 2018, China was struck by a trio of devastating natural disasters.
A powerful earthquake hit Sichuan Province, killing hundreds and leaving thousands homeless.
Floods and storms ravaged other regions, causing widespread destruction and loss of life, with total damages exceeding $19 million.

2019.tagline: China's Turbulent Year: Fierce Winds of Change
narrative: 2019 was a year of unprecedented devastation in China, as a series of intense natural disasters left a trail of destruction and loss of life. The cumulative impact of earthquakes, floods, storms, landslides, and wildfires resulted in a staggering 571 fatalities and an estimated $22.88 billion in damages. As the nation grappled with the aftermath, it became clear that the country's resilience and adaptability would be tested like never before.

2020.tagline: Turbulent skies, shaken earth, scorched land
narrative: The year 2020 saw China grapple with a trifecta of disasters. A series of intense storms ravaged the country, claiming lives and causing widespread damage. The cumulative effect of these events resulted in 463 fatalities and a staggering $27.98 billion in losses.

2021.tagline: Turbulent Skies, Shaken Earth, Fading Hope
2021.narrative: In 2021, China was struck by a trifecta of natural disasters, leaving a trail of devastation. The earthquake, storm, and flood caused widespread destruction, claiming 455 lives and inflicting estimated damages of $29,585,563,000. The drought, though less visible, had a profound impact on agriculture and ecosystems, further exacerbating the crisis.

narrative: 2022 was a year of unprecedented natural disasters in China, claiming the lives of 283 people and causing catastrophic destruction worth $17.15 million. The country was hit by a series of devastating events, including a flood that submerged entire cities, a storm that ripped through the countryside, a landslide that buried villages, an earthquake that shook the foundations, and a drought that parched the land. As the nation mourned its losses, it was left to wonder what the future held for a country so battered and bruised.

The year 2023 was marked by a series of devastating natural disasters in China, resulting in 386 recorded deaths and an estimated $30.4 billion in damages. Floods and landslides ravaged the country, while earthquakes and droughts further exacerbated the crisis. The sheer scale of the destruction left communities reeling and governments scrambling to respond.

2024.tagline: China's Turbulent Year of Nature's Fury
narrative: In 2024, China was struck by a series of devastating natural disasters, claiming the lives of 515 people and causing an estimated $19.27 billion in damages.
Floods, landslides, earthquakes, wildfires, storms, and droughts wreaked havoc across the country, leaving a trail of destruction and heartache.
As the nation mourns the loss and struggles to rebuild, the resilience and strength of the Chinese people are put to the test in the face of nature's fury.

narrative.title: Flood
narrative.text: Heavy rainfall in southeastern China caused widespread flooding in July, killing at least 150 people and affecting over 1 million others. The floodwaters destroyed homes, businesses, and infrastructure, with estimated damages reaching $0.

narrative.title: Storm
narrative.text: In October, a powerful typhoon struck eastern China, bringing strong winds and heavy rainfall. The storm caused significant damage to buildings, trees, and power lines, with at least 70 people killed and many more injured.

narrative.title: Earthquake
narrative.text: A strong earthquake struck central China in September, killing at least 50 people and injuring many more. The quake caused widespread damage to buildings, roads, and infrastructure, with estimated damages reaching $0.

narrative.title: Landslide
narrative.text: In November, heavy rainfall triggered a devastating landslide in southwestern China, killing at least 50 people and affecting many others. The landslide destroyed homes, businesses, and infrastructure, with estimated damages reaching $0.
